Here's a **tight, exec‑ready summary** you can use:

***

### What senior engineering leaders care about in developer metrics (and why)

**They want business confidence, not developer surveillance.**

**1) Delivery speed & reliability (DORA metrics)**

*   Deployment frequency, lead time, failure rate, MTTR  
    **Why:** Shows whether teams can ship changes quickly *and* safely. Strongly correlated with business performance.  
    **GitHub value:** PRs, Actions, and automation make these measurable; Copilot reduces lead time.

**2) Flow & bottlenecks**

*   PR cycle time, time to first review, WIP  
    **Why:** Bottlenecks = cost and delay.  
    **GitHub value:** PR analytics + automation → smaller PRs, faster reviews.

**3) Developer productivity & sustainability (SPACE mindset)**

*   Trends in cycle time, rework, context switching, satisfaction signals  
    **Why:** Burnout kills predictability and retention is expensive.  
    **GitHub value:** Collaboration signals live in GitHub; Copilot reduces cognitive load.

**4) AI ROI (Copilot)**

*   Adoption rate, active usage, acceptance rate, seat utilization, time saved → $  
    **Why:** Copilot is a budget line item; leaders need proof.  
    **GitHub value:** Native Copilot metrics dashboards + export to BI for ROI tracking.

**5) Quality, security & risk reduction**

*   Escaped defects, vulnerability trends, time to remediate  
    **Why:** Avoiding incidents is ROI.  
    **GitHub value:** Advanced Security + automation + Copilot‑assisted fixes.

**What not to lead with:**  
Lines of code, commits per dev, PRs per person — easily gamed and erode trust.

**One‑line positioning:**

> "GitHub metrics show whether engineering can deliver change fast, safely, and sustainably — and whether our AI investment is actually paying off."

If you want, I can turn this into **one executive slide** or a **CFO vs CTO talk track**.
